calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-11-2025 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Remove Appointed Probate Referee, Shirley C. Yawitz
Off Calendar Nov-04-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Roxanne Jen, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Michelle Uria, Executor.
Shirley Yawitz, in court, in propria persona, Probate Referee.

At the outset, Ms. Jen and Ms. Yawitz presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the instant petitions. There is discussion regarding the reported issues regarding purported inaccurate valuations provided by the Probate Referee and her pending fees.

After additional discussion, the Petition to Remove Appointed Referee is granted. Shirley Yawitz shall be removed and replaced as Probate Referee. The court shall issue an order appointing a new referee, forthwith. The matter is taken under submission with the intent to grant. The Report of Status of Administration is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Belen. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar Apr-09-2024 Continued To Jun-24-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Noelani Nasser appeared by zoom for T. Anne Lassahn (former special administrator).
Roxanne Jen appeared by zoom for Michelle Uria (petitioner).

The court noted that an inventory and appraisal has not been filed. Mrs. Jen requested a 60-day continuance to work out some discrepancies in the final inventory and appraisal that she received from the probate referee. Mrs. Nasser stated that she has no issue with a continuance. Status on close of estate is continued to June 24, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution is not ready to be filed, Counsel must file a petition for approval of a report on status of administration.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Nguyen. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-31-2024 Continued To Mar-13-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-22-2023 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-22-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance; Ethan McCallum appearing via zoom for petitioner Michelle D. Uria.

There being no further objections, and pursuant to the settlement agreement approved by this Court on March 1, 2023. The undated statutory will completed by the testator and decedent Miguel Uria, proffered by Michelle D. Uria is admitted to probate. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. Michelle D. Uria is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $1,926,000.00. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for April 09, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status on administration is filed two weeks prior to the hearing date, no appearance will be required. Shilrey Yawitz is appointed Probate Referee.

Judge: Stephen Murphy Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-06-2023 Continued To Mar-22-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-06-2023 Continued To Mar-22-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Michelle D Urias Verified Petition For Approval Of The Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Mar-01-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Rosenbaum appeared by zoom for and with Michelle Uria (petitioner) by zoom.
Aya Kanda appeared by zoom for Brenda Schenthal (respondent).
Noelani Nasser appeared by zoom for T. Ann Lassahn (former special administrator).

The court noted that there is some question regarding the signatures on the settlement agreement. Mr. Rosenbaum stated that exhibit 1 to his petition contains the settlement agreement with all of the signatures, that yesterday his client submitted a DocUsign document to him, and that mediation was conducted. Mr. Rosenbaum also stated that Elena was served with every document from San Mateo County, with every document that was filed with this court, and the mediation documents, that she was personally served at her residence in Miami, that she has never made an appearance and that they have done their due process. In addition, Mr. Rosenbaum stated that the petitioner waives an accounting by the administrator. The court waives the issue of notice. The Petition for Approval of the Settlement Agreement is under submission with the court's intent to grant the petition. The court makes a finding that the decedent's mother and sister waive their right to object and are bound by the terms of the settlement agreement. $550,000.00 is to be paid to Brenda Schenthal as the agent of Miguel Uria, Sr.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Jul-05-2022 Continued To Aug-09-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Cummins, via Zoom, for Brenda Schenthal, Petitioner; Ethan McCallum, via Zoom, for Michelle Uria, Respondent; Noelani Nasser, via Zoom, for Anne Lassahn, Special Administrator.

The court points to the thorough joint case management and confirms that there is a mediation scheduled for Jul-21-2022. Ms. Nasser confirms that two accounts have been marshalled and asks for clarification regarding additional assets. Mr. McCallum reports that be believes that his client has a vehicle that previously belonged to the decedent but needs to confirm the preceding with his client. The court advises Ms. Nasser that she should be involved with preparing the next joint case management statement. Ms. Nasser confirms that the assets in the two accounts she previously mentioned have a total value of $1.3 million dollars. The court notes that the value of the estate had been previously valued at $1.8 million dollars and that the $500k discrepancy will need to be addressed. Ms. Nasser asks whether her client will be granted the authority to liquidate stocks and manage tax issues. The court advises the parties to meet and confer regarding the preceding and to file a petition to allow additional powers to the special administrator if needed.

The Case Management Hearing is continued to Aug-09-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Prado. Not Reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Rosenbaum, via Zoom, for Michelle Uria, Petitioner; Jim Cummings, via Zoom, for Brenda Schenthal, Petitioner; James Murphy, via Zoom, for Anne Lassahn, Special Administrator.

The court and counsel discuss the status of discovery in this matter. Messrs. Rosenbaum and Cummings believe that the matter is ready for settlement discussions. The court mentions its pro bono mediation program. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed by no later than Jul-22-2022. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Jul-25-2022. The Parties are to return on Aug-09-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The court advises that today's Case Management Hearing is continued to Jul-05-2022 and a joint Case Management Statement is due fifteen days prior and should include a status of mediation. The trial date set for Jul-05-2022 is continued to Oct-18-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court confirms with Mr. Murphy and the parties that the special administrator's powers are extended to Oct-18-2022. Mr. Murphy is to submit a proposed order to the court regarding the preceding.

All matters on today's calendar are continued to Aug-09-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Attorney Jim Cummings is to monitor the Court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the minute order referring case to pro bono mediation and serve it on all parties who are entitled to notice.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-15-2022 Continued To May-17-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-15-2022 Continued To May-17-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Rosenbaum and Ethan McCallum, via ZOOM, for Michelle Uria, via ZOOM, Petitioner; Jim Cummins and Paul Barulich, via ZOOM, for Brenda Schenthal, Petitioner.

The court begins by asking if counsel plan on submitting on the papers or if they wish to supplement their petitions via oral testimony. Mr. Rosenbaum reports that he believes this matter will require an evidentiary hearing. The court queries counsel for disputed facts and legal issues in support of his belief. Mr. Rosenbaum offers testimony in advocacy for his position. Mr. Cummins offers testimony in advocacy of his position. The court notes that it doesn't appears that counsel is not ready to submit on the papers and discussions regarding the parties' positions with Mr. Cummins stating that he will submit on the papers if the court would consider them as a demurrer. The court denies Mr. Cummins' request and advises the parties that it intends to issue a Case Management Order and related dates after this hearing. Counsel and the court continue to discuss the matter with Mr. Cummins asking the court to issue a Breslin order to the parties to mediate. The court and parties discuss ADR and the court advises the parties that it will not issue a Breslin order to mediate but encourages the parties to engage in settlement discussions. Mr. Rosenbaum notes a previous filing of a petition to appoint a professional fiduciary which had been rejected by the court. The court advises Mr. Rosenbaum to re-file the petition without a hearing date as the court will provide a hearing date when the petition is efiled.

After additional discussion, a Case Management Hearing is set for May-17-2022 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Rosenbaum is instructed to monitor the Court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the Case Management Order and serve it on all parties who are entitled to notice. The matters on today's calendar are continued to the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Gomez. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-09-2021 Continued To Feb-15-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-09-2021 Continued To Feb-15-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Rosenbaum and Ethan McCallum, via ZOOM, for Michelle Uria, Petitioner; James Cummins, via ZOOM, for Brenda Schenthal, Petitioner.

The court requests status of the petition in San Mateo County. Mr. Cummins reports that both parties filed a petition, his was a competing petition, and that the San Mateo Court ruled that the original petition is moot. The court asks regarding mediation. Mr. Rosenbaum suggests that this matter requires someone to marshal the assets and that the parties previously discussed mediation prior to Maria's death. Mr. Rosenbaum suggests his client be appointed to marshal the assets. The court states that it would prefer a professional fiduciary be appointed and advises counsel to meet and confer if there is an urgency to marshal the assets. Mr. Cummins believes the court's suggestion is appropriate and that the parties may be ready to discuss settlement. Mr. Cummins advises that currently there is only one heir for Maria's estate and doesn't believe that it will be probated. The court asks if the parties know whether Maria had any other children. Ms. Muria reports that she had a son who predeceased her. The parties are instructed to file briefs regarding Michelle's petition. Mr. Rosenbaum advises that the Examiner requested the same information, and a document was subsequently filed to address that issue. The court suggests that counsel do additional diligence and that what the court looking for is something akin to a trial brief to provide legal basis regarding whether the will can be admitted or not. Counsel is instructed to file the briefs one week prior to the next calling of this matter and provide courtesy copies accordingly. The court advises counsel that if Brenda's petition is granted, the mother's estate will need to be probated. The court reiterates to counsel its instruction to meet and confer regarding a professional fiduciary and is advised to file an ex parte petition if they can agree to one.

Both Petitions are continued to Feb-15-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status on Filing of Petition for Letters of Special Administration is added for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
